description_long: This object is a double-ended, straight-bladed weapon or tool with a distinctive S-shaped central section. Both ends feature elongated, light blue blades that taper slightly, while the central grip area is black and appears to be ergonomically contoured for handling. The blades are symmetrical and lack any visible serration or edge variation, suggesting a design focused on thrusting or light slashing. The overall construction appears lightweight, with the central section possibly made of a different material for grip. There are no visible guards or ornamentation, giving the object a minimalist, utilitarian appearance. The color contrast between the blades and the handle enhances its visual clarity and functional separation.
description: A double-ended, straight-bladed weapon or tool with light blue blades and a black, contoured central grip. Minimalist and symmetrical, with no visible ornamentation or guard.
description_view_0: The object is seen from the tip, showing the narrow profile and the alignment of both blades with the central grip barely visible.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the S-shaped curve of the central grip and the full length of both light blue blades extending outward.
description_view_2: The opposite tip view, similar to view 0, highlights the symmetry and slimness of the object.
description_view_3: Another side view, mirroring view 1, clearly displaying the S-shaped handle and the straight, elongated blades.
attribute_handle_design: S-shaped, contoured, black, likely rubberized for grip
